title: Deploying a Digital Store on GitHub Pages: A Step-by-Step Setup Guide — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: Are you a digital creator from Nigeria, Ghana, or Kenya, unable to sell ebooks or courses due to PayPal restrictions? Or perhaps you're from Iran, Venezuela, or other countries with banking limitations? You're not alone. There are thousands of creatives worldwide who can't use Stripe, Gumroad, or Payhip due to geographic restrictions, KYC requirements, or banking limitations. This step-by-step guide will help you deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages that accepts crypto payments without PayPal, Stripe, or other restrictions. We'll use the Full Stack Bundle, a complete store infrastructure that includes the Core Shop Engine, Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, plus priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide, all for $79.99.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

This method works because it bypasses traditional payment processors, allowing you to collect crypto payments directly from your customers. You'll need the Full Stack Bundle to set up a store that integrates with NOWPayments, a payment processor that supports 300+ coins and has no KYC requirements. This setup will enable instant automated delivery of your digital products to customers worldwide.
What You Need Before You Start

Web3 Wallet Connect is the buyer-facing layer of your store. It generates the checkout interface that buyers see when they decide to purchase — the product display, the coin selection menu, the QR code with the exact payment amount in the buyer's chosen cryptocurrency, and the live USD equivalent price pulled from exchange rate feeds. The module handles all of this automatically. You configure it once with your product list and your wallet addresses, and it generates the correct payment details for every buyer and every coin without any manual input. The interface is clean, mobile-responsive, and designed to work for buyers who have never made a crypto payment before as well as experienced crypto users.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

GitHub Pages is the hosting infrastructure for your entire store. It is free, reliable, scales to any traffic level, and requires no server management. Your store files — the HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and any static assets — live in a GitHub repository. GitHub Pages serves them as a static website at a URL like yourusername.github.io/yourstore. There are no monthly costs, no capacity limits for a static site, and no server that can go down and take your store offline. The deployment process involves pushing your configured store files to the repository — a process that takes under five minutes once your files are ready. Updates to your store, including adding new products, are as simple as updating the relevant files and pushing to the repository.
You can bypass traditional payment processors with the Full Stack Bundle.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once your store is set up, you can go live and start selling your digital products. To make your first sale, create a new product, set a price, and configure the payment options. Then, share your store link with your audience and wait for customers to make a purchase. When you make your first sale, you'll receive the payment in your wallet, and the customer will receive the product immediately.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

To scale your store, you can upgrade to a more powerful plan, add new features, and increase your marketing efforts. You can also use analytics to track your sales and customer behavior, and make data-driven decisions to optimize your store's performance. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide to help you scale your store efficiently.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

Can I host a digital store on GitHub Pages for free? Yes, you can host your digital store on GitHub Pages for free, but you'll need to use a custom domain name and a payment processor that supports crypto payments. The Full Stack Bundle is a complete store infrastructure that includes the Core Shop Engine, Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, plus priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide, all for $79.99.
NOWPayments is a payment processor that supports 300+ coins with no KYC requirements.
Related: How Creators Like You Are Getting Paid

If you're looking for a cheap way to set up a digital product store, consider using the Full Stack Bundle, which includes everything you need to start selling digital products without PayPal, Stripe, or other restrictions. You can also check out our shop at https://unchained-commerce.github.io/Shop/index.html to see how it works. The Full Stack Bundle is perfect for solo developers building crypto-native digital product stores.
Your Action Checklist

To deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages, follow these steps: create a new GitHub repository and add the Core Shop Engine to it; install the Admin Dashboard and Wallet Connect using npm or yarn; configure the Admin Dashboard to connect to NOWPayments; set up a payment gateway and configure delivery options; test the system to ensure smooth transactions; create a new product, set a price, and configure payment options; share your store link with your audience and wait for customers to make a purchase.
The Full Stack Bundle includes priority updates, Discord support, and a deployment guide to help you scale your store efficiently.
Full Stack Bundle — $79.99. Everything — Core Engine, Wallet Connect, Dashboard — plus priority updates, Discord support, and deployment guide. Your complete store infrastructure, one payment.
